assertion.text: Erdős Problem #510: declared status 'open'. Formalized: yes. If $A\subset \mathbb{Z}$ is a finite set of size $N$ then is there some absolute constant $c>0$ and $\theta$ such that\[\sum_{n\in A}\cos(n\theta) < -cN^{1/2}?\] Current best: Ruzsa \cite{Ru04} (improving on an earlier result of Bourgain \cite{Bo86}), proved an upper bound of\[-\exp(O(\sqrt{\log N})).\]Polynomial bounds were proved independently by Bedert \cite{Be25c} and Jin, Milojevi\'{c}, Tomon, and Zhang \cite{JMTZ25}. The best bound follows from the method of Bedert \cite{Be25c}, which proved the existence of some $c>0$ such that, for all $A$ of size $N$,\[\sum_{n\in A}\cos(n\theta) < -cN^{1/7}.\]The example $A=B-B$, where $B$ is a Sidon set, shows that $N^{1/2}$ would be the best possible here. Prize: no. Tags: analysis.
